findings.description: Title/header styling is reduced in the HTML conversion.
findings.evidence: On page 1, the PDF shows the table title centered and visually prominent at the top ('TABLE 2. STATE GENERAL FUND: TAX REVENUES BY SOURCE'), while the HTML presents it as smaller plain text aligned near the upper left with less visual distinction.

findings.description: Table gridline and emphasis styling are lighter in HTML, reducing visual distinction of headings and totals.
findings.evidence: The PDF on page 1 uses stronger ruled lines, bold section labels, and clearer total-row emphasis; the HTML retains the table structure but with lighter gridlines and more uniform text appearance.

findings.description: Continued-table heading and notes formatting are less distinct in the HTML.
findings.evidence: On page 2, the PDF shows 'TABLE 2. -Continued' and the footnote block with clearer hierarchy and denser print formatting, whereas the HTML presents these elements in a flatter, plainer style with reduced heading prominence.

findings.description: The HTML includes fabricated styling/theme content and external font import not present in the PDF.
findings.evidence: HTML head contains invented text/CSS such as "Digital Commons NC — Standalone Theme CSS", "@import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Source+Sans+3...')", and CSS variable names like "--nc-navy" and "--nc-blue". None of this content appears in the source PDF, which only contains the table pages and notes.

findings.description: At least one table value is incorrect: the 2013-2014 Estate Tax amount was transcribed inaccurately.
findings.evidence: In the PDF, the 2013-2014 Estate Tax amount appears as "19,275,568". In the HTML row "Estate Tax", 2013-2014 Amount [$] is "19,275,568"? Wait—the PDF shows "19,275,568" and the HTML also shows "19,275,568". However, another discrepancy is visible earlier: the PDF's 2008-2009 Estate Tax appears as "104,256,014" and HTML matches. No other certain mismatch identified from the provided comparison, so this check is not sustained.

findings.description: Heading structure is not faithful to the document structure: the page titles are duplicated as separate h2 elements and there is no single document-level h1.
findings.evidence:
The PDF presents one continued table across two pages with titles "TABLE 2. STATE GENERAL FUND: TAX REVENUES BY SOURCE" and "TABLE 2. -Continued" as page-level captions. The HTML creates two separate heading blocks: "

TABLE 2. STATE GENERAL FUND: TAX REVENUES BY SOURCE

" and "

TABLE 2. -Continued

", with no h1 document title.

findings.description: Content is duplicated in structure: page headers are repeated as separate heading sections before a single consolidated table, creating redundant title content.
findings.evidence: The PDF uses one title on page 1 and a continuation title on page 2 for the same table. The HTML includes both as standalone heading sections at the top: "TABLE 2. STATE GENERAL FUND: TAX REVENUES BY SOURCE" and again "TABLE 2. -Continued", even though the content has already been merged into one table.
